title: Fermented Springy Saudi-Arabian Red Bean Paste Tagine
description: A flavorful, fermented, and springy dish inspired by Saudi-Arabian cuisine, featuring anko-red-bean-paste and cooked to perfection in a slow-cooked tagine.
instructions: Combine the red bean paste, brown rice paste, fermented garlic, parsley, fenugreek leaves, salt, black pepper, turmeric, cardamom powder, and cayenne pepper in a bowl.
Mix well until you get a uniform paste.
Heat the coconut oil in a saucepan over medium-low heat.
Add the paste to the saucepan and cook for 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Transfer the mixture to a slow cooker and cook on low for 8 hours.
Once the tagine is cooked, serve it hot with some fresh parsley sprinkled on top.
Garnish with additional fenugreek leaves if desired.
Serve with some brown rice or flatbread on the side.
This tagine is best served with a dollop of yogurt or sour cream to balance the flavors.
Refrigerate the leftover tagine for up to a week and reheat it as needed.
Note: To make fermented garlic, peel 2-3 cloves and let them sit in a jar covered with water at room temperature for 24-48 hours.
Strain the garlic and rinse it with water before using in the tagine.
